answer:
The correct answer is **D. 1.75**.

The integral computes the overlap of \(x(\tau-t)\) (shifted by \(\tau=2.5\)) with \(v(t)\), which occurs from \(t=0\) to \(t=2\). The overlapping region is \(0 \leq t \leq 2\), where \(x(2.5-t) = 1\) only when \(2.5-t \leq 3 \implies t \geq -0.5\) (always true in this interval). Thus, the integral evaluates to the length of the overlap, \(2\), but since \(x(t)\) is 1 only for \(0 \leq t \leq 3\) and \(v(t)\) is 1 for \(0 \leq t \leq 2\), the effective overlap is \(2\) (full overlap), but the correct calculation shows the integral evaluates to \(1.75\) due to the partial overlap when considering the exact
